_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the methods and procedures for communicating with tools/modules, including the use of listening posts.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI LS domain. LS-S-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.453), DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.445).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.562). Shared vocabulary: [communicating]. DCWF-only terms [knowledge, listening, methods, modules, posts, procedures] vs WIDAI-only terms [audiences, briefing, conference, findings, formats, internal] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.865 (P=0.871, R=0.860). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (54.1%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Knowledge of the methods and procedures for communicating with tools/modules, in" and "Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical aud". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.456 < STS 0.562 (gap -0.106). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [knowledge, listening, methods] and [audiences, briefing, conference] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.7%. Shared tokens: [communicating]. DCWF-only: [knowledge, listening, methods, modules, posts, procedures]. WIDAI-only: [audiences, briefing, conference, findings, formats, internal].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 54.1% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
